stages.title: Site Analysis
stages.narration: Stage 1. Site analysis. Timber frame structures are particularly suitable for seismic mountainous regions due to their lightweight and flexible behavior.
stages.explanation: Timber frame structures are particularly suitable for seismic mountainous regions due to their lightweight and flexible behavior.

stages.title: Setting Out
stages.narration: Stage 2. Setting out. Column locations and wall centerlines are marked.
stages.explanation: Accurate layout ensures correct frame geometry and load transfer.

stages.title: Excavation
stages.narration: Stage 3. Foundation trenches are excavated to competent bearing strata.
stages.explanation: Excavation reaches competent bearing strata for safe load transfer.

stages.title: Foundation Construction
stages.narration: Stage 4. PCC, stone foundation, and the RCC plinth beam are constructed.
stages.explanation: The foundation distributes structural loads and provides a stable base.

stages.title: Timber Treatment
stages.narration: Stage 5. Timber members receive preservative treatment and protective coating.
stages.explanation: Timber treatment improves durability and protects against moisture, insects and fungal attack.

stages.title: Timber Column Installation
stages.narration: Stage 6. Timber columns are erected and connected to the plinth beam.
stages.explanation: Timber columns form the primary vertical load-carrying system.

stages.title: Timber Beam Installation
stages.narration: Stage 7. Timber beams connect columns and form the primary frame grid.
stages.explanation: Beams transfer roof and wall loads into supporting columns.

stages.title: Timber Bracing System
stages.narration: Stage 8. Timber bracing members are now being installed. These diagonal elements resist horizontal earthquake forces and significantly improve frame stability.
stages.explanation: Bracing resists horizontal earthquake and wind forces.

stages.title: Timber Wall Frame
stages.narration: Stage 9. Secondary wall framing is installed to support laths and plaster.
stages.explanation: The wall frame supports laths and plaster while contributing to lateral stability.

stages.title: Timber Lath Installation
stages.narration: Stage 10. Horizontal timber laths are installed across the wall frame.
stages.explanation: Laths provide support for plaster and improve wall integrity.

stages.title: Wire Mesh Installation
stages.narration: Stage 11. Wire mesh is attached to laths to control cracking and improve composite action.
stages.explanation: Wire mesh controls cracking and improves composite action.

stages.title: Plaster Application
stages.narration: Stage 12. Cement-lime plaster is applied in base and finish coats.
stages.explanation: Plaster forms a lightweight enclosure while protecting the structure.

stages.title: Roof Frame Construction
stages.narration: Stage 13. Timber trusses, rafters and purlins form the lightweight roof frame.
stages.explanation: Lightweight roof systems reduce seismic demand.

stages.title: Roof Covering
stages.narration: Stage 14. CGI roofing sheets are installed and fastened to the frame.
stages.explanation: Proper anchorage protects against wind uplift.

stages.title: Doors, Windows and Finishing
stages.narration: Stage 15. Door and window frames and final finishes are completed.

stages.title: Completed Structure
stages.narration: Construction complete. The timber frame absorbs and redistributes earthquake energy through flexible deformation.
stages.explanation: Completed timber frame with lath and plaster house ready for occupancy.

timber_column.title: Timber Column
timber_column.compressionBehavior: Carries vertical loads from beams and roof to foundation.
timber_column.loadTransfer: Transfers compression through base connection to plinth beam.

timber_beam.title: Timber Beam
timber_beam.bendingBehavior: Resists bending from roof and wall loads.
timber_beam.structuralRole: Connects columns and distributes loads horizontally.

timber_brace.title: Timber Brace
timber_brace.earthquakeResistance: Provides diagonal resistance to lateral seismic forces.
timber_brace.lateralStability: Stabilizes frame against sway and racking.

timber_lath.title: Timber Lath
timber_lath.wallSupportFunction: Supports plaster layers and maintains spacing for composite wall.

wire_mesh.title: Wire Mesh
wire_mesh.crackControl: Limits plaster cracking under movement and shrinkage.
wire_mesh.compositeAction: Improves bond between plaster and timber substrate.

roof_truss.title: Roof Truss
roof_truss.forceDistribution: Triangulated members distribute roof loads to wall plate.
roof_truss.triangulation: Truss action provides efficient span and stability.
